result: Separator-sharing is a THRESHOLD effect, not a dose-response. Star topology (one separator shared across `fanout` children), per=4 scarce, 5 seeds kato15: block deficit is large (~0.16-0.28 R2) whenever a separator is shared, but PLATEAUS across fanout 2..32 -- does NOT grow monotonically. PARTIAL REFUTATION of the stated 'more sharing -> monotonically bigger gap' hypothesis. MF R2 == DENSE throughout.

novelty_classification.note: Corrects a hypothesis I stated after E6. Sharing MATTERS (confirmed); degree-of-sharing beyond 2 does NOT add much (refuted). Recorded per 'multi-seed is the verdict / don't force the convenient narrative' -- single-seed (seed 0) looked monotone, 5-seed does not.

guards_added: junction_tree::star_tree_equals_dense_with_shared_separator (MF == dense on a star; assembles many Schur messages into one parent)
framework_impact.refines: F-EVO-8: coupling value is a THRESHOLD on sharing (bounded by per-clique data budget), NOT a dose-response in number of sharers

corrections.post-E6-hypothesis: I stated the scarce-regime gap would 'widen further' with more separator-sharing. Multi-seed shows it JUMPS then PLATEAUS -- monotone growth NOT supported. Corrected here.

honest_scope: additive-in-features linear target; single scarcity per=4, sep=3
small d at low fanout is noisy (d=9 at fanout=1; one seed had BLOCK beat MF via over-regularization)
genuinely non-additive cross-clique feature still untested

next_experiment_proposal.name: genuinely non-additive cross-clique feature (explicit product spanning a separator) -- the last open discriminating knob; joint scarcity x sharing at larger d
